summary: Strong fall‑'71 performance with standout jams (The Other One, Wharf Rat) captured in a crisp yet not top‑tier recording.
review: Reviewers praise the show’s energy and transitional vibe, highlighting solid renditions of Bertha, Sugaree, Playin’ in the Band and especially The Other One flowing into Me & My Uncle and Wharf Rat. The recording is described as crisp and clear by one reviewer but several note that another version sounds better, suggesting only fair fidelity.

ai_rating.rationale: The musical performance is consistently praised with multiple standout songs, while recording quality receives mixed feedback; the weighted average yields a solid above‑average rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: One reviewer calls it "remarkably crisp and clear," but two others say another version sounds better, indicating decent but not exceptional fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical early‑'70s structure – short rock tunes in set one, long exploratory jams in set two. The Other One segues into Me & My Uncle and then into a delicate yet muscular Wharf Rat, showing smooth transitions and confident pacing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mentioned resetting the count during The Other One intro, reflecting his leadership; no specific guitar tone remarks.
band_member_comments.Phil: Sings high harmonies, contributing to the vocal confidence of the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Carries most rhythm duties while Keith embellishes on piano; his rhythm work is noted as solid.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux’s piano adds sophistication but isn’t fully integrated yet, providing tasteful accents.
